description_long: This is a multi-tiered pagoda structure with five distinct stories, each separated by wide, overhanging eaves that curve gently upward at the corners. The main body of the pagoda is painted a vibrant red, while the roofs are a dark, muted brown, creating a striking contrast. The building features open balconies on each level, with railings and support beams visible beneath the eaves. At the very top, a pointed ornamental finial extends upward, emphasizing the verticality and elegance of the design. The overall form is symmetrical, with each tier slightly smaller than the one below, giving the structure a tapered silhouette. The architectural style and details are reminiscent of traditional Chinese pagodas, often found in temple complexes or as landmarks in urban parks.
description: A five-story red and brown pagoda with upward-curving eaves, open balconies, and a pointed ornamental finial, reflecting traditional Chinese architectural style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical five-tiered structure, prominent balconies, and the central entrance area.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view, still frontal, with the same features as view 0 but with subtle shadow differences.
description_view_2: Another frontal view, nearly identical to views 0 and 1, with minor lighting and shadow variations.
description_view_3: Frontal view similar to the others, maintaining the clear visibility of all five stories and the central finial.
